Package cityrescue

Class Unit

java.lang.Object
cityrescue.Unit
Direct Known Subclasses:
Ambulance, FireEngine, PoliceCar
public abstract class Unit extends Object
Unit.java handles common unit behaviour shared by emergency vehicles. Depicts a rescue unit's type, including whether it is an ambulance, a fire engine or a police car, handling each unit type depending on how they respond to it. Status indicates whether a unit is Idle, en route, at the scene or out of service. Tracks work progress for each unit type. This abstract class manages a unit's ID, the unit type, the location as a coordinate, the status and finally the work tick, which is used to track progress across an incident resolution.

  • Constructor Details

    • Unit

      public Unit(int id, UnitType type, int x, int y, int homeStationId)
      Constructs a unit
      Parameters:
      id - this is the unique identifier for the unit
      type - the type of unit ("ambulance", "fire engine" or "police car")
      x - the x coordinate for the location of the unit
      y - the y coordinate for the location of the unit
      homeStationId - the identifier of the starting point, home station

      Returns the identifier for the unit

      y - new y coordinate of the unit's location

      public int getIncidentId()
      Returns the ID of the incident associated with the unit or -1 if no incident.

      incident ID, or -1 if not assigned

      incidentId - ID of the incident associated with the unit

      Clears the assigned incident from the unit

      Returns the ID of the station the unit starts in

      public void setHomeStationId(int stationId)
      Sets the home station for a unit

      Returns the number of work ticks of a unit whilst at the incident

      public void incrementWorkTicks()
      Increments work ticks for the unit tracking progress whilst the unit is resolving an incident.

    • resetWorkTicks

      public void resetWorkTicks()
      Resets work ticks to zero, whenever a unit starts resolving a new incident or is idle

    • canHandle

      public abstract boolean canHandle(IncidentType type)
      Determines whether the unit can handle the incident in question.
      Parameters:
      type - the type of incident
      Returns:
      true/false depending on whether the unit can handle the incident

    • getTicksToResolve

      public abstract int getTicksToResolve(int severity)
      Returns the number of ticks required to resolve an incident for a given severity level.
      Parameters:
      severity - the severity of the incident
      Returns:
      number of ticks to resolve that incident
